problem.summary: Compute FP32 scaled dot-product attention forward, O = softmax(scale * Q * K^T + mask) * V, for contiguous [batch, heads, sequence, head_dim] tensors. The baseline assigns one thread to each output element and recomputes the softmax row for every value dimension. The optimized seed assigns one CTA to each query row, stages K/V key tiles in shared memory, and uses FlashAttention-style online softmax statistics to avoid materializing the full attention matrix.
inputs: float* q: row-major contiguous [batch_size, num_heads, query_length, head_dim]
float* k: row-major contiguous [batch_size, num_heads, key_length, head_dim]
float* v: row-major contiguous [batch_size, num_heads, key_length, head_dim]
float* output: row-major contiguous [batch_size, num_heads, query_length, head_dim]
outputs: For each batch, head, and query position, output equals the stable softmax-weighted sum over visible V rows
constraints: FP32 inputs, FP32 accumulation, and head_dim == value_dim in this seed
Contiguous B,H,S,D layout only; no ragged batches, padding masks, dropout, rotary embedding, paged KV cache, or grouped-query attention yet
The optimized seed is intended for head_dim <= 128 and uses a fixed 32-key tile with one CTA per query row
Causal masking treats query positions as aligned to the suffix of the KV sequence, so query_length=1,key_length=N models decode at the latest token
The default 1x4x128x128x64 causal shape is a ROCm timing target; no ROCm result files are currently attached

verification.method: The harness computes a CPU double-precision stable attention reference for the same scale and causal mask, then reports max_abs_error, max_rel_error, bad_count, and passed. Ported from the CUDA corpus; rerun on ROCm hardware before marking passed.

curation.notes: Ported from the CUDA corpus on 2026-05-20. CUDA-origin timing artifacts were removed; rerun on ROCm hardware before making performance claims.
